preamble: Elision. The voiced velar fricative [ɣ] is lost, often resulting in compensatory lengthening of the preceding vowel or merging into a diphthong. This process is common during the reduction of velar sounds across languages.

examples.note: Old English to Middle English loss of velar fricatives (e.g. historical development of 'high', 'nigh').

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
